title: padus avium var. discolor
intro: Padus avium var. discolor, also known as the purple-flowering dogwood or Cornelian cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The Cornelian cherry has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits of boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation. Herbal preparations of the Cornelian cherry include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make syrup or jam from its fruit, as well as extracts used in pharmaceutical products.
